acceptance_targets: [CH18_AWARD_ENDING_MONTAGE] After snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:279, the revised chapter produces one new irreversible collective state the reader can name, and the final scene shows either an on-page intersection between principal arcs or a direct consequence of Yusuf's publication. If Lena still boards the Lausanne train at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:331, that movement is paid off on page rather than left as an implied off-screen convergence.

[CH18_CRAFT_PUBLICATION_RECAP] In the revised passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:317, the reader learns at least one fact, decision, or obstacle that was not already available by snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:277. The scene no longer spends multiple sentences restating that the release is not proof but enables outside scrutiny; instead it ends on a distinct Lena-specific change in situation.

[CH18_DIALOGUE_THERAPY_DIAGNOSIS] By the end of the revised therapy passage at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:341, the session has changed Margot's treatment situation in a concrete way or exposed a misunderstanding with immediate stakes. At least one of Margot's major responses arrives with interruption, restart, refusal, or other spoken friction instead of another complete diagnostic formulation, and the later body-hunger evidence at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:407 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:427 remains the clearest demonstration of the craving.

[CH18_PROSE_TEMPLATE_REUSE] After revision, no more than one of the four cited sites still uses the 'with the [quality] of someone who...' scaffold. Each replacement reads as a distinct concrete observation tied to the current focalizer, not as another abstract summary of what a minor character is presumably like.
[R010] Ch18's first 80 lines contain no more than 1 reference to Kai's wrist or shoulder pain.
[R025] Ch18 contains no more than 7 instances of the word 'something.'
[C003] Ch18 line 6422 refers to the empty air above Margot's right wrist.

findings.acceptance_test: After snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:279, the revised chapter produces one new irreversible collective state the reader can name, and the final scene shows either an on-page intersection between principal arcs or a direct consequence of Yusuf's publication. If Lena still boards the Lausanne train at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:331, that movement is paid off on page rather than left as an implied off-screen convergence.
findings.problem: The chapter's irreversible public turn lands when Yusuf publishes and the inquiry begins, but the final chapter does not shape the aftermath into a capstone state change. After that publication beat, the book disperses into parallel codas: Lena boards a Lausanne train without an on-page payoff, and the novel finally closes on Margot privately surrendering her phone. For a last chapter, this reads as status montage rather than a chosen final configuration of the novel's central relationships and pressures.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the post-publication sequence at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:317, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:331, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:431 so the ending creates one convergent final turn. Either pay off Lena's move toward Lausanne on page with Kai and/or Yusuf, or make the last scene's action directly caused by Yusuf's public release instead of functioning as an adjacent private coda. If Margot remains the closer, tie snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:431 to the day's public fallout, not just to her standing treatment routine.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:317, the reader learns at least one fact, decision, or obstacle that was not already available by snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:277. The scene no longer spends multiple sentences restating that the release is not proof but enables outside scrutiny; instead it ends on a distinct Lena-specific change in situation.
findings.problem: Lena's changing-room section mainly retranslates what Yusuf's section has already made legible: the release is not proof, outside researchers can now inspect the work, and Farah's prospects move from impossible to maybe. Because the chapter spends a second POV pass paraphrasing the same public turn, the scene adds atmosphere and tenderness but not enough new consequence to justify its structural weight.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:317 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:327, replace recap with a new local turn for Lena: a decision, contact, destination, obstacle, or other consequence that was not already visible by snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:277. If the train beat stays at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:331, make its purpose operationally legible enough to count as new dramatic work rather than a teaser appended to summary.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of the revised therapy passage at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:341, the session has changed Margot's treatment situation in a concrete way or exposed a misunderstanding with immediate stakes. At least one of Margot's major responses arrives with interruption, restart, refusal, or other spoken friction instead of another complete diagnostic formulation, and the later body-hunger evidence at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:407 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:427 remains the clearest demonstration of the craving.
findings.problem: The Helga scene repeatedly turns Margot's addiction into composed diagnosis. Helga asks thesis-prompt questions and mini-speech diagnostic questions, and Margot answers with several polished formulations of the same truth before the Eisbach material finally renders the hunger in the body. That makes the office exchange sound written-about rather than spoken-through, and it cleans up the roughness the style bible wants in Margot's private register.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the office exchange at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:345, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:353, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:391, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:397 so it does one thing the river scene cannot: create a treatment consequence, misclassification, restriction, or sharper misunderstanding. Let Margot's speech fight for language rather than delivering serial finished diagnoses, and preserve snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:407 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:427 as the chapter's primary proof of craving.

findings.acceptance_test: After revision, no more than one of the four cited sites still uses the 'with the [quality] of someone who...' scaffold. Each replacement reads as a distinct concrete observation tied to the current focalizer, not as another abstract summary of what a minor character is presumably like.
findings.problem: The chapter reuses the marked descriptive scaffold 'with the [quality] of someone who...' four times across unrelated scenes. At this frequency it stops reading as choice and starts reading as narratorial autopilot, flattening the distinct observational texture of Kai's and Lena's sections.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast at least three of snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:3, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:11, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:19, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:315 into more local, concrete observation rather than the same inferential template. Use POV-specific sensory noticing or behavior in place of another generalized mind-reading clause.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch18's first 80 lines contain no more than 1 reference to Kai's wrist or shoulder pain.
findings.evidence: Line 6020: 'His left wrist had been complaining since lunch. He turned it once. The ache stayed.'; line 6030: 'his shoulder settling in its socket with a smoothness that still made him aware of the joint'; line 6078: 'a short shrug that didn't involve the bad shoulder'
findings.problem: Kai's wrist/shoulder ache is re-proved 3 times within 58 lines of ch18 (6020-6078). This is the 12th+ manuscript-wide re-statement of the same chronic condition. None of these instances add materially new information about the injury.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep one injury reference in ch18's opening -- line 6020 or 6078, whichever best serves the scene's emotional register. Cut the other two. By the final chapter, the reader carries this knowledge; a single touch is sufficient.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch18 contains no more than 7 instances of the word 'something.'
findings.evidence: 'something' appears 14 times in ch18 (4,959 prose words), density 2.26 per 800 words. Manuscript total: 110 in 84,498 words (1.04/800w, threshold 1.0/800w). Ch18 is the densest chapter for this hedge.
findings.problem: Ch18 uses 'something' 14 times -- the highest of any chapter and more than double the threshold density. As the final chapter, excessive hedging undercuts the resolution's emotional clarity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Audit each 'something' in ch18. Replace 7-8 instances with the specific noun, sensation, or state the hedge is avoiding. Retain 'something' only where genuine indeterminacy is the point of the sentence.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch18 line 6422 refers to the empty air above Margot's right wrist.
findings.evidence: Ch02 line 324: 'right wrist'; Ch10 line 2890: 'right wrist'; Ch18 line 6422: 'the empty air above her left wrist where the feed band had sat for years'
findings.problem: Ch18 line 6422 places the phantom feed band on Margot's LEFT wrist. The canonical placement is the right wrist (ch02, ch10, ch12). This is a second instance of the same left/right inconsistency.
findings.rewrite_direction: In ch18 line 6422, change 'her left wrist' to 'her right wrist' to match the established canonical placement.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
